summary: Israeli forces have full operational freedom to target Hezbollah tunnels around Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge, conducting over 150 strikes since midnight. Hezbollah accuses Israel of violating the ceasefire, while a U.S. source reports a ceasefire agreement to begin at 16:00. Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu reiterates that Israeli troops will remain in southern Lebanon as long as needed, warning of a heavy price for Hezbollah.
key_points: Israeli troops have "full freedom of action" to demolish major Hezbollah tunnel systems at Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
The IDF reports striking more than 150 Hezbollah targets in Lebanon since midnight (Source: @manniefabian/58634).
Hezbollah claims Israel has never honored any ceasefire agreement and accuses it of massacres against Lebanese civilians (Source: @presstv/194804).
A senior American official told Reuters that Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire starting at 16:00 (Source: @asafroz/50520).
Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Israeli forces will stay in southern Lebanon "as long as necessary" and will impose a heavy price on Hezbollah (Source: @VahidOnline/76516).
Jet activity was observed over Baghdad, Iraq (Source: @shin_persian/7134).
Iranian officials discussed postponing a planned meeting in Switzerland, indicating ongoing diplomatic activity (Source: @kianmeli1/88088).
U.S. messages indicate Israel agreed to overlook Hezbollah violations as part of ceasefire talks (Source: @inon_yttach/9068).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli forces have full operational freedom to target Hezbollah tunnel infrastructure at Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge, conducting over 150 strikes since midnight. Hezbollah alleges Israel has repeatedly violated the ceasefire and accuses it of civilian massacres. A U.S. source reports a ceasefire agreement to commence at 16:00, while Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu vows to keep troops in southern Lebanon as long as needed.

points: Iranian officials postponed a scheduled meeting in Switzerland, reflecting ongoing diplomatic negotiations related to the regional conflict (Source: @kianmeli1/88088).

Iranian media quoted a national security official claiming Iran shifted responsibility for containing Israeli military activity onto the United States during recent negotiations (Source: @SharghDaily/166933).
Iranian statements warned that it may not adhere to the first clause of a ceasefire concerning Lebanon, indicating potential non‑compliance (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371712).
Iran discussed a $300 billion reconstruction fund tied to sanction exemptions, highlighting broader economic pressures but not direct combat actions (Source: @VahidOnline/76514).

IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin said Israeli troops have "full freedom of action" to eliminate threats anywhere, focusing on tunnel demolition at Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
The IDF reported striking more than 150 Hezbollah targets in Lebanon since midnight, indicating a high tempo of operations (Source: @manniefabian/58634).
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu declared Israeli forces will remain in southern Lebanon "as long as necessary" and warned of a heavy price for Hezbollah attacks (Source: @VahidOnline/76516).
Israeli Defense Minister Katz affirmed the army will stay in Lebanon and respond to any attack with significant force (Source: @VahidOnline/76516).
A senior American official told Reuters that Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire starting at 16:00, suggesting a diplomatic de‑escalation step (Source: @asafroz/50520).
Hezbollah spokesperson stated Israel never complied with any ceasefire and accused it of massacring Lebanese civilians (Source: @presstv/194804).
Israeli forces continue operations in southern Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah tunnel networks and command centers (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
Lebanese reports cite 18 civilian deaths from Israeli attacks across the country (Source: @VahidOnline/76516).
Hezbollah leader Sheikh Naim Qassem emphasized unity among Lebanese resistance factions amid ongoing clashes (Source: @presstv/194800).
An Israeli soldier from unit 52 was reported killed in combat in Lebanon (Source: @pkpoi/142879).
Jet activity was observed over Baghdad, Iraq, indicating possible aerial operations in the region (Source: @shin_persian/7134).
U.S. airstrikes targeted three vehicles in the Jabal al‑Sheikh area of western Aleppo countryside, Syria (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371721).
Iran issued a new online clearance system for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, reflecting heightened maritime security measures (Source: @presstv/194790).

key_developments: Israeli troops have full operational freedom to destroy Hezbollah tunnel systems at Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge, with over 150 strikes reported since midnight (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
Hezbollah alleges Israel has repeatedly violated the ceasefire and committed civilian massacres in Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194804).
A senior U.S. official confirmed Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire effective at 16:00, indicating a potential de‑escalation (Source: @asafroz/50520).
Prime Minister Netanyahu pledged Israeli forces will remain in southern Lebanon as long as needed, warning of a heavy price for Hezbollah (Source: @VahidOnline/76516).
Jet activity over Baghdad suggests possible aerial operations or heightened alert in Iraq (Source: @shin_persian/7134).
U.S. airstrikes in western Aleppo targeted three vehicles, showing continued engagement in Syrian theater (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371721).
Iran introduced a compulsory online clearance system for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, affecting regional maritime traffic (Source: @presstv/194790).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah's tunnel infrastructure remains a high‑value target for Israeli forces, prompting intensive air and ground strikes in southern Lebanon. Israeli commitments to maintain a prolonged presence increase the risk of further escalation, especially if Hezbollah continues to contest tunnel demolitions. U.S. diplomatic signals of a ceasefire at 16:00 may temporarily reduce hostilities, but competing narratives from both sides suggest fragility in any agreement.
sections.regional_response: Iran's diplomatic maneuvers, including postponing talks in Switzerland and issuing new maritime clearance rules, indicate a focus on strategic leverage rather than direct military escalation. Gulf states are monitoring the Strait of Hormuz developments, while Iraq observes increased jet activity over Baghdad, reflecting broader regional security concerns. International actors, notably the United States, are mediating ceasefire terms while warning Israel against further violations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future Israeli operations are likely to concentrate on remaining Hezbollah command centers in the Beaufort and Ali Taher highlands. Monitoring of Hezbollah tunnel repair activities will be critical to assess compliance with any ceasefire. U.S. communications suggest a window for diplomatic de‑escalation, but any breach could trigger rapid Israeli retaliation. Iranian maritime regulations may affect logistics for regional actors, and the observed jet activity over Baghdad warrants close air‑defense surveillance.
